title: invitations — Eloquent observers
description: Model observers registered by `InvitationsServiceProvider::boot()`. Each observer's hooks[] enumerates the Eloquent events it acts on plus the side effect. Idempotent by construction — every side effect checks state before firing.

hooks.description: Generate the ULID+prefix id if unset. Generate a 256-bit random token via random_bytes(32), hash SHA-256 for token_hash + take first 8 hex chars for token_prefix. The RAW token is stashed on the model as a non-persisted attribute (via forceFill in a controlled way) so it can be plucked once for the outbound message. Set default expires_at = now() + 14 days if unset. Set default state = pending.
Write the initial InvitationEvent { event: created, occurred_at: created_at } (audit trail baseline). Dispatch SendInvitationJob unless channel=link (no message sent for link-only).

Refuse updates that mutate token_hash, token_prefix, target_type, target_id, application_id, tenant_id — these are immutable. Refuse state regressions (e.g. accepted → pending). Refuse mutations when state is terminal, except for the resend flow (which is handled through the resend service, not direct save).

For each state transition, write a matching InvitationEvent row. Dispatch the corresponding domain event (InvitationDelivered / InvitationAccepted / etc.). Idempotent — checks the state actually changed before firing.
Soft-delete. Emit a metric `invitations.soft_deleted.total`. No event fires — soft-delete is an ops action, not a domain state transition.
